commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=74808089847687bb930a34a0d103791951b357f3)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a MiniMax fetch-based provider with env-based configuration, provider dispatch/tests, and a README provider-list entry.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: the new direct HTTP `fix` method can only return JSON, while current `fixCommand` records a non-failing provider result as an applied patch based on snapshots. I did not run it because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed because the contributor must repair or limit the provider surface and add real MiniMax proof for the final behavior; automation cannot prove their external account setup.

target.title: feat(provider): add minimax provider for MiniMax Token Plan models
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a new `minimax` provider that calls the MiniMax OpenAI-compatible
chat completions endpoint directly via `fetch()`. This unblocks clawpatch
users with a MiniMax Token Plan when their default `codex` provider is
unavailable, rate-limited, or simply not their preference.

Closes #125.

## Behavior

- Provider name: `minimax` (dispatched alongside `codex`, `acpx`,
  `grok`, etc.)
- Default model: `MiniMax-M3` (selectable via `--model`)
- Default endpoint: `https://api.minimax.io/v1` (Token Plan base URL;
  `api.minimaxi.com` and `api.minimax.chat` are wrong for Token Plan keys
  — see MiniMax provider docs in hermes for the host/endpoint matrix)
- Output is enforced via `response_format: {type: "json_schema", ...}`
  for the four JSON-schema-validated operations (`map`, `review`, `fix`,
  `revalidate`)
- Read-only review + read-only revalidate; write-capable fix follows the
  same `readOnly` flag pattern as the other providers
- Connectivity check is a `GET /v1/models` with a 30s timeout

## Configuration

| Env var | Required | Default | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `MINIMAX_API_KEY` | yes | — | Token Plan API key (`sk-cp-…`) |
| `MINIMAX_BASE_URL` | no | `https://api.minimax.io/v1` | Endpoint override |
| `MINIMAX_MODEL` | no | `MiniMax-M3` | Default model |
| `CLAWPATCH_MINIMAX_TIMEOUT_MS` | no | `300000` | Per-call timeout |
| `CLAWPATCH_PROVIDER_TIMEOUT_MS` | no | `300000` | Fallback timeout |

## Usage

```bash
# Defaults (MiniMax-M3)
clawpatch review --provider minimax --feature feat_xxx

# Pick a different model
clawpatch review --provider minimax --model MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed --feature feat_xxx

# Override endpoint (e.g. for proxy or staging)
MINIMAX_BASE_URL=https://proxy.example.com/v1 clawpatch review --provider minimax --feature feat_xxx
```

## Implementation

- New helpers in `src/provider.ts`:
  `minimaxTimeoutMs`, `minimaxBaseUrl`, `minimaxDefaultModel`,
  `minimaxFailureMessage`, `runMinimaxJson`.
- New provider object `minimaxProvider` with the 5 standard methods
  (`check`, `map`, `review`, `fix`, `revalidate`).
- New dispatch case in `providerByName` between `grok` and `pi`.
- Exposed the 4 pure helpers via `__testing` for unit tests.
- README provider list updated alphabetically.

## Tests

11 new unit tests in `src/provider.test.ts` covering:

- `providerByName("minimax")` dispatch
- `minimaxTimeoutMs` env-var precedence
  (`CLAWPATCH_MINIMAX_TIMEOUT_MS` > `CLAWPATCH_PROVIDER_TIMEOUT_MS` > default)
- `minimaxBaseUrl` default and override
- `minimaxDefaultModel` default and override
- `minimaxFailureMessage` classification for 401, 429, and generic
  statuses

`pnpm test src/provider.test.ts` reports **146/146 passing** (135
pre-existing + 11 new).

## Verification

```text
pnpm typecheck          # clean
pnpm lint               # clean (1 unused-param warning fixed by renaming root -> _root)
pnpm format:check       # clean (after pnpm format)
pnpm test src/provider.test.ts   # 146/146 passed
pnpm build              # clean
```

Live connectivity test against the real `/v1/models` endpoint with a
valid Token Plan key returns
`provider=minimax default-model=MiniMax-M3 base=https://api.minimax.io/v1`.

End-to-end review against `feat_library_d4f04bd776` (a real feature in
`ferminquant/budget`) completed in 4m07s and produced 5 schema-valid
findings with concrete `evidence.path` + `startLine`/`endLine` references
and `suggestedRegressionTest` payloads.

## Pre-existing test failures (unrelated)

The full `pnpm test` run shows 19 failures in `src/workflow.test.ts`
that exist on a clean `main` (verified by stashing this change and
re-running). They are unrelated to the provider change and not addressed
by this PR.

## Security considerations

- The provider never logs the API key or any response body in plaintext.
  Error previews use `safeProviderPreview(body, 200)` which limits
  exposure to a truncated body fragment.
- `response_format.json_schema` enforces structured output, reducing
  the risk of injection from model output.
- No new file-system or network surfaces are added beyond the chat
  completions endpoint.
target.author: ferminquant

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
